scheme_name: Bharati Scheme for Education Professional Education Course - Chartered Accountancy Final
page_title: Bharati Scheme for Education Professional Education Course - Chartered Accountancy Final

details: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) launched the "Bharati Scheme for Education Professional Education Course - Chartered Accountancy Final" for the academic year to encourage Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh to continue their education. Under this scheme, eligible poor Brahmin student applicants will receive financial assistance for the academic year. This scheme applies to the Brahmin students of Andhra Pradesh covering all 13 districts.
benefits: Financial Assistance for the Professional Education Course (Chartered Accountancy Final): ₹20,000/-.
eligibility: The student and his/her parents should belong to the Brahmin community.
The student's parents should stay in Andhra Pradesh.
The annual family income of parents/guardians should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
The student should have cleared both groups of CA-Inter (IPCC) after 2014 and registered and paid fees for CA final exam.
The student should not have benefitted from Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (APBWC) for the same exam (CA Final) earlier.
The student should be enrolled in the Praja Sadhikara Survey (PSS).

online: Click on "Registration" under the concerned scheme name.
Fill out the application form with required details like:
Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etc
Upload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details
Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etc
Receive the reference number after the submission.
Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "
Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status.
Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details.
Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" under the concerned scheme.
Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme".

documents_required: Aadhaar Card of the Student & Parents (with address in Andhra Pradesh).
White Ration Card / Income Certificate Number (issued by Mee Seva).
Proof of clearance of IPCC (both groups).
Proof of registration and fees paid for the CA Final exam.
Andhra Bank/ SBI Passbook in the name of Student.
For students studying outside A.P. account in any Nationalized Bank is acceptable.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the BSE?
faqs.answer: To support Brahmin students in continuing their education.

faqs.question: Is there any specific income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: Yes, family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/- per annum.,

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance disbursed?
faqs.answer: Directly into the student's bank account through online transfer.

faqs.question: How is eligibility verified?
faqs.answer: Through Mee Seva, Praja Sadhikara Survey, and Civil Supplies department data.

faqs.question: Is there an age limit for applicants?
faqs.answer: No specific age limit is mentioned in the eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum percentage requirement?
faqs.answer: No minimum percentage requirement is specified.

faqs.question: Is there a restriction on the number of children per family applying?
faqs.answer: No, as long as each applicant meets the eligibility criteria individually.

faqs.question: Can applicants submit handwritten documents?
faqs.answer: No, all documents must be scanned and uploaded in PDF format.

faqs.question: Is there an interview process for selection?
faqs.answer: No, selection is based on the eligibility criteria and documents submitted.

faqs.question: Can students with arrears apply?
faqs.answer: No, students must have passed all subjects in previous years.
